other_observers: Found by Cathy Sheeter, who texted me, and then we observed/photographed bird together until it flew away
description: General appearance like that of a female Mallard with a rather dark body contrasting sharply with paler tan-brown head and neck. Crown dark brown as was line through eye, contrasting with pale head. Legs reddish. Tail brown. Blue-ish speculum with relatively narrow white borders. Size equivalent of male Mallard, not female. Bill olive with some yellow hues. No curled tail feathers. Uppertail coverts mottled.Behavior: Mostly sleeping on island in pond.Call: Note heardPlumage: Male
